page: Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and a 50 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 102 GB of local storage. No GPU resources are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the E4_v3 sits on the lower end of the compute spectrum. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with large-block read and write operations scoring in the bottom 10%, while small-block cached reads reach 158.31 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25%. Database operations and Passmark CPU tests also register in the bottom 25%, reflecting its older hardware generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4_v3 offers a high memory capacity relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute or high-speed memory throughput. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable, albeit modest, processing power. This instance is best suited for legacy enterprise applications, small relational databases, caching layers, or lightweight memory-bound workloads that benefit from a 32 GB memory footprint without demanding modern CPU architectures.
description: Microsoft Azure E4_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates modest processing capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth performance scoring in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. Despite these performance constraints, the instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This hardware profile makes the E4_v3 a cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance compute, such as small databases, caching layers, and legacy enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for legacy database and caching workloads.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 102 GB of local storage
